content: Global population aging represents unprecedented demographic shift—people 65+ years will outnumber children under 5 by 2030. By 2050, 1 in 6 people globally will be over 65 (up from 1 in 11 in 2019). Healthy aging defined by WHO as developing and maintaining functional ability enabling well-being in older age. Functional ability comprises intrinsic capacity (physical/mental capacities), environment (home, community, society), and their interaction. Compression of morbidity hypothesis suggests delaying disease onset can reduce lifetime disability burden, though evidence is mixed. Key health concerns: multimorbidity (average 3+ chronic conditions), geriatric syndromes (falls, frailty, delirium, incontinence, pressure ulcers), cognitive impairment/dementia, social isolation, polypharmacy. Ageism remains significant barrier to healthy aging. Life course perspective recognizes aging trajectories shaped by cumulative advantages/disadvantages across lifespan.
